name: Empty Set
guide_hints: In this level, we will prove the existence of the empty set. We will prove that there exists a set A that contains no elements: `‚ąÉA ‚ąÄx (x‚ąČA)`.
We can prove this by using the Axiom of Specification to construct a set of all elements that are not equal to themselves.
First, we define a set B (the set from which we will construct our empty set) and our variables. Type `cv B A x`.
Now we write our specification formula: `cf thm1 ‚ąÉA ‚ąÄx (x‚ąąA ‚áĒ (x‚ąąB ‚ąß x‚Č†x))`.
Apply the specification axiom to prove this: `apply thm1 specification`.
Now we can introduce the existential variable `S` from `thm1`. Type `intro thm1 S`. This yields `‚ąÄx (x‚ąąS ‚áĒ (x‚ąąB ‚ąß x‚Č†x))`.
Now start a mission to prove our goal: `mission goal`.
Since our goal is an existential statement, we must introduce a specific set that satisfies it. Let's introduce our specific set `S`. Type `intro S g2` (this makes the new goal `g2: ‚ąÄx (x‚ąČS)`).
Now we need to prove this property for an arbitrary element. Let's introduce a fresh variable `y`. Type `intro y g3` (this introduces `y` and makes the new goal `g3: y‚ąČS`).
Expand our goal so it uses standard notation: `rw "notin" g3`. The goal is now `¬¨(y‚ąąS)`.
Now let's use our arbitrary element `y` in our proven theorem. Type `intro thm1 y thm1_y`. This yields `y‚ąąS ‚áĒ (y‚ąąB ‚ąß y‚Č†y)`.
Expand `neq` in our theorem to see the standard notation: `rw "neq" thm1_y`. This yields `y‚ąąS ‚áĒ (y‚ąąB ‚ąß ¬¨(y=y))`.
We know from Axiom E1 that every element is equal to itself. Claim this: `cf E1y y=y` and prove it: `apply E1y E1`.
Now we have `thm1_y: y‚ąąS ‚áĒ (y‚ąąB ‚ąß ¬¨(y=y))` and `E1y: y=y`. These imply `¬¨(y‚ąąS)` (which is `g3`).
Apply propositional logic to close the mission: `apply g3 PC1 thm1_y E1y`.
Congratulations! You have proven the existence of the empty set.
To define the empty set notation for future use, you can write: `ct "‚ąÖ" őĶ ?v1 ‚ąÄ?v2 (?v2 ‚ąČ ?v1)`.
